title: The dysmorphic newborn with fused toes and a low cholesterol
blurb: A malformation syndrome from a block in the LAST step of cholesterol synthesis — the rare disease where cholesterol is too LOW.

patient.complaint: Multiple congenital anomalies noted at birth; ambiguous genitalia.

opening: Term newborn with microcephaly, ptosis, a small upturned nose, micrognathia, 2-3 toe syndactyly, postaxial polydactyly, and ambiguous genitalia (46,XY undervirilization). He feeds poorly with a weak cry. Total cholesterol is strikingly LOW for a neonate, and plasma 7-dehydrocholesterol is markedly elevated. What do you want to ask, examine, or order next?
history: First child of non-consanguineous parents (autosomal recessive). Intrauterine growth restriction. Prenatal ultrasound suggested anomalies. No maternal statin use. Family history unremarkable.
exam: Microcephaly, bitemporal narrowing, ptosis, anteverted nares, cleft palate, micrognathia. 2-3 toe cutaneous syndactyly, postaxial polydactyly. Hypospadias with undervirilized genitalia. Hypotonia.

prompts.cause: Which enzyme (final cholesterol-synthesis step) is deficient?
prompts.mechanism: Briefly: why do malformations occur (think Sonic hedgehog), and why is cholesterol LOW here when most sterol cases are about excess?

pinpoint.prompt: Click the cholesterol-synthesis span whose final step (7-DHC reductase) is deficient here.
pinpoint.correctMsg: Yes — the late cholesterol-synthesis pathway (squalene → … → cholesterol). DHCR7 normally performs the final reduction of 7-dehydrocholesterol to cholesterol; its loss leaves cholesterol LOW and 7-DHC HIGH. Cholesterol is required to covalently modify and activate Sonic hedgehog signaling, so deficiency causes the multi-organ malformations.
pinpoint.wrongMsg: Not quite. The defect is at the very END of cholesterol synthesis (converting the immediate precursor to cholesterol).
pinpoint.revealMsg: Target: the squalene → cholesterol span (DHCR7 is the final step). 7-dehydrocholesterol accumulates; cholesterol is low — the opposite of familial hypercholesterolemia.

mcqs.stem: Why does cholesterol deficiency cause congenital malformations?
choices: Cholesterol is required for covalent modification and activity of Sonic hedgehog, a key developmental morphogen
Cholesterol is needed for neural crest migration only
Low cholesterol causes apoptosis of all dividing cells
mcqs.explanation: Sonic hedgehog is cholesterol-modified to signal properly. Without cholesterol, hedgehog patterning fails — producing the holoprosencephaly-spectrum facial features, limb anomalies (syndactyly/polydactyly), and genital undervirilization of SLOS.

mcqs.stem: How is SLOS conceptually opposite to familial hypercholesterolemia despite both being 'sterol' disorders?
choices: SLOS is a cholesterol SYNTHESIS deficiency (low cholesterol); FH is an LDL-receptor clearance defect (high LDL cholesterol)
mcqs.explanation: SLOS fails to MAKE cholesterol (DHCR7) → low cholesterol, high 7-DHC. FH fails to CLEAR LDL (LDLR) → high plasma LDL. Same pathway map, opposite ends — a nice teaching pair.

mcqs.stem: What is the rationale for dietary cholesterol supplementation in SLOS?
choices: To raise tissue cholesterol and reduce toxic 7-DHC accumulation, improving growth and some symptoms
mcqs.explanation: Cholesterol supplementation (often with egg yolk or purified cholesterol) aims to replenish the deficient sterol and suppress 7-DHC; benefits are partial. Avoid drugs that further inhibit cholesterol synthesis. Statins are used cautiously/experimentally to lower 7-DHC in milder cases.
